articles.title: Rupee opens 1 paise lower at 94.69 against US dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee opened weaker at 94.69 against the US dollar, down 1 paise. Market focus has shifted from oil prices to expectations of a US Federal Reserve rate hike in September, impacting Asian currencies including the rupee.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Waterways Leisure Tourism IPO Day 1: Issue opens today. Check GMP, review, key dates. Apply or not?
articles.summary: The Waterways Leisure Tourism IPO opens from 23-25 June, offering fresh shares worth ₹585 crore. It has attracted ₹263.25 crore from anchor investors, with a price band set at ₹769-808 per share. The company operates Cordelia Cruises, a leading domestic ocean cruise brand.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Sebi’s municipal bond push may not bring retail rush, experts warn of credit risks
articles.summary: Sebi has cut the minimum ticket size for municipal bonds to ₹10,000 from ₹1 lakh and eased rules to deepen the municipal debt market. But experts say low investor awareness, patchy liquidity and difficulty assessing municipal credit risk will keep retail participation muted in the near term.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.summary: Market expert Raja Venkatraman shares his top stock picks for 23 June. Here’s his technical outlook and trade strategy.

articles.title: Retail traders may have lost over ₹1 trillion in F&O again in FY26
articles.summary: Top brokers who submitted client profit and loss data on equity derivatives say that while the count of smaller investors could have fallen due to regulatory tightening,  overall losses would be similar to last fiscal year's ₹1.05 trillion, a fact buttressed by exchange turnover data
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: TCS to declare Q1FY27 results, interim dividend on this date. Check details
articles.summary: Tata Consultancy Services will review Q1FY27 financial results on July 9, 2026, and consider an interim dividend payment. TCS has a history of consistent dividend distribution and reported a net profit increase of 12.22% for March 2026 quarter amid a declining stock value.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Kirloskar Oil Engines shares hit 20% upper circuit. JM Financial sees more upside
articles.summary: Kirloskar Oil Engines shares surged 20% after winning a substantial order from HyperNext for 96 units of its 2,500 kVA power systems, enhancing its position in the data centre market dominated by Cummins. JM Financial and Motilal Oswal raised target prices, citing strong growth potential.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India deserves a different valuation framework in the current regime, says Delorean Partners' Amit Bhartia
articles.summary: Amit Bhartia suggests India's equity market requires new valuation standards rather than relying on historical averages. He emphasizes the importance of assessing current economic contexts instead of comparing price-to-earnings ratios, cautioning against misleading conclusions drawn from past data.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Bharat Electronics shares to be in focus on Tuesday as company secures ₹1,081 crore order
articles.summary: Bharat Electronics has secured additional orders worth ₹1,081 crore, enhancing its revenue visibility. The company's stock is attracting attention following prior orders and discussions of Indian defense sales to the UAE, contributing to a notable demand increase for India's defense systems.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India's investor base tops 130 million, but only one in 10 trade
articles.summary: Of the 131 million registered investors, only 12.9 million traded at least once in May across equity cash and derivatives segments.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: US stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq futures slip as US-Iran talks ease oil prices
articles.summary: US stock futures were mixed on June 22 amidst uncertainty over Middle East peace talks. S&P 500 futures fell 0.2%, while the Dow dipped 29 points. Investors are cautious as tech stocks pause after gains, awaiting clarity on US-Iran diplomatic efforts.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Jio Platforms flags talent retention as a key risk to IPO success
articles.summary: Jio Platforms warns that losing skilled personnel in AI, cybersecurity and network engineering could harm its business.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Top Gainers & Losers on June 22: Kirloskar Oil Engines, Delhivery, Gabriel India, Urban Company among top gainers
articles.summary: The Indian stock market closed with gains as US-Iran peace talks progressed, boosting pharmaceutical and technology stocks. The Nifty 50 rose 0.37% to 24,102, while the Sensex closed at 77,044, up 0.31%. Broader markets and various sector indices also recorded positive movements.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stock market today: Sensex gains 300 points, Nifty 50 ends above 24,100; what moved the stock market today?
articles.summary: The Sensex ended 291 points, or 0.38%, higher at 77,094, while the Nifty 50 settled at 24,102.90, up 90 points, or 0.37%. The Nifty Midcap 100 and the Nifty Smallcap 100 indices rose by 0.34% and 0.60% higher, respectively.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Can NSE IPO, Jio Platforms IPO impact liquidity in the Indian stock market?
articles.summary: Jio Platforms and the National Stock Exchange (NSE) are set to launch major IPOs, raising billions. Jio's IPO could reach $4 billion, and the NSE aims for around ₹30,000 crore, raising concerns about liquidity impacts on the secondary market.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Power Mech Projects shares gain 5% as company secures ₹1,009 crore JSW Thermal Energy contract
articles.summary: Power Mech Projects shares rose 5% after securing a ₹1,008.90 crore order from JSW Thermal Energy for civil and structural works in West Bengal. The contract, to be completed in 36 months, enhances the company’s order book amid a series of recent large contract wins.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Will Singapore remain India's export star even after Iran war ends?
articles.summary: West Asian geopolitical tensions have driven a surge in India’s exports to Singapore, utilizing its robust transshipment infrastructure to diversify from traditional hubs. Will this continue?
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Govt plans subsidized tomato sales to cool price surge
articles.summary: In several local retail markets, tomatoes are currently being sold at ₹60-70 per kg. Retail tomato prices have jumped in India due to delays in arrivals and weather-related disruptions in producing regions.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: US trade chief to visit India for interim deal discussions
articles.summary: The visit comes amid efforts by India and the US to deepen economic ties and address market access issues through a comprehensive trade pact.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: RBI governor asks banks to treat MSMEs as long-term partners
articles.summary: The RBI governor asked financial institutions to make greater use of India's digital public infrastructure
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: RBI flags risks if West Asia peace deal fails, says India remains strong
articles.summary: RBI's assessment comes amid easing tensions between the US and Iran, which has helped moderate concerns around energy supply disruptions and global trade routes
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: India's core sector growth hits seven-month low as Iran war bites
articles.summary: Given the tepid core sector output in May 2026, India's IIP growth is likely to weaken to 2-3% in the month from 4.9% in April 2026, according to an analyst.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Gen Z is out-earning millennials, shows data; but has inflation already closed the gap?
articles.summary: New research shows Gen Z workers are out-earning millennials at the same age — but with soaring rents in the UK, US, and India, higher wages may not mean better lives. Here's what the data really shows.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: From village adulteration to organized food crime: The evolution of India’s fake milk industry
articles.summary: Evidence is emerging that in some northern states and Gujarat people are being sold fake milk that looks and tastes like the real thing. This deep dive looks at the growing sophistry in adulterating milk and how consumers can protect themselves.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: The benefits and risks of RBI’s policy on foreign currency NRI deposits
articles.summary: To shore up the rupee, the government is looking at NRIs to make foreign currency deposits in Indian banks. This has implications for India’s external debt management.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India, Iran look to soon resume talks to get Chabahar Port plan back on track
articles.summary: India and Iran may resume discussions on restoring normal operations at the strategic Chabahar Port and reviving plans to expand the facility, as geopolitical conditions in the region show signs of stabilising, according to two people aware of the matter.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Young Punjab kids ‘rich for 4 yrs after selling their lands… then broke’: World Bank's Ajay Banga on urban agri economy
articles.summary: World Bank President Ajay Banga pointed out challenges in urban and agricultural economies, particularly in Punjab, where youth selling farmland quickly spend their earnings, leading to financial instability.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Views, virality and CJP protest: How India's creator economy found a new stage at Jantar Mantar
articles.summary: A May 2025 report, ‘From Content to Commerce: Mapping India’s Creator Economy’ by BCG, said that with over 2–2.5 million monetised content creators influencing more than $350–400 billion in consumer spending, the ecosystem is projected to drive $1 trillion+ in creator-influenced consumption by 2030.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: The week in charts: State debt concerns, WPI inflation, NSE’s IPO debut
articles.summary: In this weekly Plain Facts compilation, we present to you data-based insights, with easy-to-read charts, to help you delve deeper into the stories reported by Mint in the week gone by.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Development finance should be used strategically to improve governance, service delivery in Northeast: FM Sitharaman
articles.summary: Finance minister Nirmala Sitharaman said that externally aided project funding to India's Northeast has risen nearly eightfold to ₹76,000 crore since 2014, and urged states to use such finance to strengthen governance and service delivery.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: AI boom shields Asia from West Asia shock, but risks mount for India and region: Moody’s Analytics
articles.summary: Moody's Analytics has said that AI-led exports and investment have helped Asia-Pacific weather the fallout from the West Asia conflict, but higher oil prices, inflation, trade tensions and weather-related disruptions could slow growth in the years ahead.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Watchful and wary: Inside MPC's unanimous decision to hold rates
articles.summary: At its 3-5 June meeting, the Monetary Policy Committee kept the key repo rate unchanged at 5.25%, while acknowledging that there were considerable risks to its assessments of inflation and growth.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Oil slides below $80 as US-Iran peace deal eases supply fears
articles.summary: Brent slipped below $80 a barrel as the US and Iran signed a peace framework, easing supply concerns and paving the way for the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Fuel curbs rollback likely as Iran deal raises hope
articles.summary: The Centre is weighing a rollback of emergency curbs on fuel sales imposed during the West Asia conflict after the US and Iran sued for peace, a move that could ease pressure on farmers, lower inflation and improve prospects for the world's third-largest oil importer.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India secures steel market access under UK FTA; exporters to avail benefits from 15 July
articles.summary: India has secured quota-based protection for its steel exports under the India-UK Ceta, ensuring market access is preserved even as Britain's safeguard measures take effect from 1 July. The broader trade pact will come into force on 15 July.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Net direct tax collections rise 14.6% to ₹5.21 trillion as of 17 June
articles.summary: Net direct tax collections rose 14.64% to ₹5.21 trillion as of 17 June, led by a 22.5% surge in corporate tax receipts and a near-45% jump in STT collections.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Centre plans to tap worker cess to fund on-site housing for construction workers
articles.summary: The housing ministry is also evaluating a policy to unlock empty airspace above roads and transit lines, helping developers overcome land shortages in dense urban areas.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Basmati rice exports to West Asia poised for rebound after US, Iran sign peace deal
articles.summary: The reopening the Strait of Hormuz is expected to mitigate shipping bottlenecks, boosting trade to the Gulf, which represents a major market for Indian basmati rice.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: As war pressures ebb, edible oil prices may ease 10% ahead of the festive season
articles.summary: India could see edible oil prices fall by up to 10% in coming months as easing West Asia tensions, lower freight costs and higher palm oil supplies from Indonesia and Malaysia improve availability. However, benefits may take time to reach consumers due to existing inventories and supply contracts.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: With Iran-US peace deal in sight, IEA projects oil supply surplus in 2027
articles.summary: Global crude oil supply is set to fall by 3.9 million barrels per day (bpd) to 102.4 million bpd this year amid trade disruptions due to the West Asia war, before rebounding by 8 million bpd next year, the International Energy Agency (IEA) said on Wednesday.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Crude price fall may not last as nations rush to rebuild reserves
articles.summary: Even with a US-Iran peace deal on the horizon, global crude oil prices are not expected to plummet due to a significant supply shortfall.
articles.label: BEARISH
